note: Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. This CME is affected by a data gap in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 from 2025-06-30T17:06Z-19:06Z caused by a scheduled calibration in SOHO LASCO.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. The leading edge becomes diffuse about halfway through the SOHO LASOC C3 field of view.
Best fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. Partially obstructed by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Best fit measurement using several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. CME front is much more defined and visible in later frames of LASCO C2 into C3 than its initial presentation around the LASCO C2 occulting disk.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and the faint observation in STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery. Due to the faint nature of this front, some spread in the longitude is possible around E80-E100.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and the faint observation in STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery. Due to the faint nature of this front, some spread in the longitude is possible around E80-E100.
Updated measurement using SOHO/STEREO A and now including GOES CCOR out to 12 solar radii.
Measurement using best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. CME half-width is less than 10 degrees.
One spacecraft measurement assuming some modest westward deflection of about 5 degrees and starting nearby to the source of the eruption in EUV imagery.

Measurement using best fit among STEREO A COR2, SOHO LASCO C3, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The location of the source and northern deflection seen in UV imagery were taken into consideration. The right-hand side of this CME is blocked by the pylon in C3 while the left-hand side of this CME is blocked by the pylon in CCOR-1. This CME is not obstructed at all in STEREO A COR2.

Measurement using best fit between a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 and consideration of source location observed in UV imagery. There is a high degree of uncertainty with this measurement due to the overall faintness and irregular shape of the CME in both SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is confidence that this feature is indeed a CME, however, due to the presence of a faint flux rope feature discernable in both coronagraphs. This CME is not discernable in GOES CCOR-1 and is blocked by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This event measured with a half width less than 10 degrees.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the very faint front with three coronagraphs. The source is far-sided.
Best fit measurement using an abundance of frames from SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. This CME is obstructed by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1 running difference imagery.
Best fit measurement using SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and one frame of STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This front is quite faint and diffuse, making tracking the leading edge difficult.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The outer LE is more visible in SOHO imagery, but the bright ejecta is observed well in both coronagraphs.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Due to overlap with bright CME 2025-07-08T04:24Z and its far northern latitude, the longitude range may vary E90-E120.

Best fit measurement of extremely faint leading edge using a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and several frames of STEREO A COR2. Not visible in SOHO LASCO C3 and not trackable in GOES CCOR-1 due to faintness of leading edge.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude may vary slightly as the source location is likely far-sided and unknown.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint front with three coronagraphs. There seems to be a significant northwestern deflection happening when comparing the latitude and longitude of the CME with the approximate location of the source - a filament eruption centered around (N12W15).
Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs. No clear source has been found in EUV imagery.

Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C2, C3, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. GOES CCOR-1 was not used for this fit due to faintness of leading edge and matching front used in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Analysis based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT and corroborated by the location of the CME source (an eruption of filament in the SW quadrant).
Speculative one-coronagraph (SOHO LASCO C2) analysis based on the location of the likely source eruption (AR 4134).
Speculative one-coronagraph 3D analysis based on the suspected source location (dimming centered around -52 deg).
Approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint front with 3 coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found for this CME in available EUV imagery.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 and on visible source.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 and on visible moving field lines from beyond the NW limb.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 and on visible source.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on source location seen in available UV imagery of the disk and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 running difference imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Triangulated measurement of the leading edge between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2, capturing the full width of the eruption with the southern lobe slightly ahead of the western lobe. Only later frames of C3 and COR2A are used due to observed acceleration when including earlier frames of C2/C3 and COR2A.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. CME is observed widening, deflecting northward, and accelerating beyond early frames, so only later frames of C3 and COR2A are used for best fit.
Measurement based on limited early frames due to CME's faintness. Measurement based on potential source location.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. CME travels into LASCO C3 pylon and is not well visible in LASCO C2.

Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is some uncertainty with these parameters due to the irregular shape of the leading edge of the CME (which may possibly be two overlapping CMEs from the same or similar source area) and the faintness of the event.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source of this CME has not been found in EUV imagery and is likely farsided.

Very approximate analysis based on the fit (of the poorly defined, very narrow front) with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the location of the likely CME source (the flare and eruption in AR 4135), although there seems to be a 5 degree deflection towards the west from the source location. The width of the CME is likely lower than the tool's default 10 degrees.

Very approximate analysis based on the best fit in all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).  This analysis is confirmed by the location of the possible CME source (minor eruption at ~S18E40), however there should have been an eastern deflection if this source is correct.
Triangulation between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source. There was an additional leading feature visible in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 that could not be consistently tracked in STEREO A COR2.

Approximate analysis based on the fit of the two-pronged front with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). This analysis is confirmed by the location of the likely CME source, which appears to be or or just behind the east limb.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured longitude reflects eastern deflection from the far-sided source. Multiple measurements produced longitudes ranging from -70 to -63 degrees.
Measurement parameters based on visible source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on visible source location and on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on visible source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on visible source and on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. There is a higher level of uncertainty due to faintness of event.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on location of candidate source observed in available UV imagery of the Earth-facing disk as well as best fit of the faint leading edge using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2. The leading edge becomes diffuse before reaching the SOHO LASCO C3 or GOES CCOR-1 field of view. This CME was not captured in real time by STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began 2025-07-23T17:53Z.

Measurement based on location of source observed in available UV imagery of the Earth-facing disk as well as best fit using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and C3. The leading edge is too diffuse to reliably track in GOES CCOR-1. This CME was not captured in real time by STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap which began 2025-07-23T17:53Z.

Measurement based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured longitude may vary slightly as a result.
Uncertain measurement/simulation with just one spacecraft (STEREO A) using three frames, using knowledge of the source location.

As the CME is not seen in STEREO A coronagraph, this is a somewhat speculative one-coronagraph analysis based on SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery only and on the very minor possible source seen in SDO AIA 193.
A very approximate analysis of this unclear fuzzy front in two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2)

Best fit measurement using a few early frames of SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. The leading edge is diffuse in all coronagraph imagery, which adds some uncertainty to this measurement.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Best fit measurement using a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs. The fit can be done with a range of longitudes.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source.
Measurement based on visible, on-disk source location.
An uncertain one-coronagraph analysis with STEREO A COR2 based on the location of the possible source (AR 4157) and on a very faint front in STEREO A COR2 coronagraph.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 and on visible source location.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). This analysis is confirmed by the location of the likely CME source - an eruption just behind the SW limb.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Due to the faint, diffuse nature of this leading edge, some variance in all parameters may exist as tracking the true leading edge is difficult. High confidence in the <10 degree HW.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Leading edge becomes too diffuse to track about halfway through the STEREO A COR2 field of view.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. The early propagation of the CME in SOHO LASCO C2 is covered by a data gap.

A very uncertain analysis based on the likely far-sided source - an opening of field lines on/behind the west limb in STEREO A EUV 195 seen after 03:55Z (indicating a longitude of >=133 deg) and on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). As the on/behind the limb in STEREO A longitude doesn't produce a good fit, the analysis with the longitude of 124 producing a decent fit has been selected. However, a decent fit can be produced with the longitude as low as 110 degrees.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. This CME is most clear in STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best fit among SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs, SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The source is likely behind the W limb.

Updated measurement based on the approximate source location. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, a triangulated measurement was not possible. The measured longitude may vary slightly as a result.

Updated measurement based on the approximate source location, which is observed slightly beyond the eastern limb. Due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery, a triangulated measurement was not available. The measured longitude may vary slightly as a result.

Approximate analysis made with one coronagraph only (SOHO LASCO) and based on the location of the likely source - eruption in Active Region 14154. No STEREO COR2A imagery is available for this time frame because of large STEREO A data gap, so no 2-coronagraph analysis is possible.
Measurement based on the source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. This CME is directed into the pylon in both SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Approximate analysis made with more imagery. Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME seems to be deflecting northeastward from the location of the Active Region/flare/eruption location, as is consistent with the proximity of the coronal hole that is located southeastward of the location of the Active Region.
Measurement based on potential source location using STEREO A COR2 imagery only.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location.

Measurement based on best fit with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT focusing on measuring the northernmost portion of the leading edge. The CME may deflect west from the source location. This CME measurement is more approximate than usual, due to lack of visibility in STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO C2 and STEREO A COR2 of the CME which overlaps the earlier CME: 2025-08-06T17:12Z.
Leading edge measurement using SOHO and STEREO A; triangulation seems to suggest the eruption is associated with near-limb eruptive activity.
Leading edge measurement using triangulation between SOHO and STEREO A to determine an approximate source longitude.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. This CME was likely deflected Westward due to proximity to a coronal hole.

Measurement based on location of candidate source and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Not visible in STEREO A COR2, possibly due to directionality if candidate source is the true source.
Measurement of the bulk of the CME based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 running difference imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as source location.
Measurement using the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as the source location near/beyond the east limb in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement using the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as the source location near/beyond the east limb in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT as well as source location near/beyond east limb.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1, as well as location of the source eruption seen in available UV imagery of the disk.
Best fit measurement of the BULK using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1, as well as location of the source eruption seen in available UV imagery of the disk.
Measurement using fit between several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Parameters based on best fit. Deflection from potential source likely due to nearby coronal hole.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The CME in GOES CCOR-1 was too diffuse/faint to use in this measurement.
Measurement based on approximate source location and few frames in SOHO LASCO C2 before CME fades. True half width is less than 10 degrees.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Leading edge measurement out to 7 solar radii before the CME fades away abruptly, especially in SOHO LASCO.
Triangulation of the leading edge using SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Not seen well in SOHO LASCO C3.

Measurement of the bulk trying to incorporate as much of the CME as possible in SOHO LASCO and STEREO A, not incorporating some materials which escapes/accelerates out ahead of the leading edge, especially along the sides.
A very approximate analysis based on the imperfect fit with two coronagraphs, SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by location of the far-sided source, as seen from EUVI A.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement of faint leading edge using knowledge of source location and best fit between a handful of frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and one frame of GOES CCOR-1.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The front is too diffuse/faint to use in GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Best fit measurement using several frames of GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. This CME is not captured in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery due to a data gap in SOHO LASCO lasting from 2025-08-12T00:54Z to 2025-08-12T04:40Z.

Measurement based on source signature from former AR 14168 (~N05W96). There is a significant amount of uncertainty in this measurement due to the lack of triangulation and the proximity of the source to a large coronal hole.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT. Limited imagery was used due to the CME's faintness.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Approximate analysis based on the approximate location of the associated filament (just behind the NW limb) and on the fit with two coronagraphs, SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. The longitude could range from 93 to 115 degrees (based on the fit).
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.
Better triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to the faintness of this event in STEREO A COR2 imagery, and likely deflection from the source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

Approximate one-coronagraph analysis with STEREO A COR2 coronagraph only, based on the likely location of the source - an eruption of a filament approximately centered at long=-70 deg. This analysis is not confirmed by fitting with another coronagraph, since there is a planned outage of SOHO and this CME is NOT seen in CCOR-1.
Updated measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulated measurement with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3
This is an initial measurement with only GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Preliminary measurement based on imagery from STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. At the time of measurement, the CME was in one obstructed frame of SOHO LASCO C3.
Preliminary measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1

Shock measurement encompassing the outermost leading edge of the halo; width greater than 60 degrees required to fit the entire shape in all coronagraphs, but as the width was increased beyond conventional halo widths (45-to 55), the lemniscate began to fit the CME more skillfully.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STERE A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2.
Preliminary measurement of BULK. Parameters based on best fit using initial frames available in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Preliminary analysis of SHOCK. Parameters based on GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR1 as well as visible source location on W limb in STA EUVI.
Best-fit measurement based on triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. This CME is obscured by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO/GOES CCOR and STEREO A in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C3/GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as likely source location.
Measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 in SWPC_CAT, as well as source location.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement based on source location as well as best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement using early frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and limited available STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.
Measurement of the leading edge using triangulation with up to 25 degrees of uncertainty in the source longitude.
Analysis based on the location of the source eruption and fully confirmed by the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2).
A very approximate uncertain analysis based on the likely source (southern eruption) and on the fitting the fuzzy unclear fronts with all 3 coronagraphs(SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by the location of the source - the flare somewhat eastward of the location of AR 14199.
Preliminary triangulated fit using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement of the leading edge using triangulation between SOHO C2, C3 and STEREO A COR2A.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.

Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and confirmed by what we know about the likely far-sided source from coronal signatures - moving field lines in SDO AIA 171 and an ejecta from behind the NE limb in SDO AIA 304.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1).  No source has been found for this CME in available EUV imagery.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
A super uncertain analysis based on the fit of the very fuzzy faint front with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). The source has not been found in EUV imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. No source has been found for this event to confirm this analysis.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2); not confirmed by a source as no clear source found in EUV imagery.
Measurement based on candidate source location using STEREO A COR2 frames only. Not visible in CCOR-1 due to faintness. Not captured by SOHO LASCO due to a data gap which began 2025-08-27T12:30Z.
Best fit measurement using several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. Not captured by SOHO LASCO due to a data gap which began 2025-08-27T12:30Z.
Best fit measurement using several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. Not captured by SOHO LASCO due to a data gap which began 2025-08-27T12:30Z.
Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.

Best fit measurement using several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. Leading edge is extremely faint in GOES CCOR-1, adding some uncertainty to the measurement. Not captured in real time by SOHO LASCO due to a data gap which began 2025-08-27T12:30Z.
Measurement based on the best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This measurement is speculative as the source is likely far-sided, but a far-sided fit was difficult to obtain.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is far-sided and a precise source location is unknown, the measured parameters may vary slightly.

A very approximate analysis based on the fit of this very faint front with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). There seems to be an eastward deflection from the more western source going on.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Best fit measurement using several frames of STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. This CME was captured by only one frame of SOHO LASCO C2 in real time, due to a regularly scheduled downlink data gap which began 2025-08-28T20:24Z.

Best fit measurement using several frames of GOES CCOR-1 and a few frames of STEREO A COR2. The leading edge is extremely diffuse, adding some uncertainty to this measurement. This CME was captured by only one frame of SOHO LASCO C2 in real time, due to a regularly scheduled downlink data gap which began 2025-08-28T20:24Z.

Single spacecraft 3D measurement with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to lack of visibility in other coronagraphs, and the observed source around S12W65. Longitude could vary W60-W70. Half width less than 10 degrees.

Single spacecraft 3D measurement with SOHO LASCO C2 only due to lack of visibility in other coronagraphs, and the observed source around S12W75. Longitude could vary W70-W80. Half width less than 10 degrees.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as a visible source signature beyond the limb from STEREO A.
Measurement using candidate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 frames.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. No clear source is observed on-disk here, but good confidence in the coronagraph triangulation on front-sided parameters near E78.

Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1. There is some uncertainty in this measurement due to the faintness of the front and lack of good source candidate.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. Not visible in CCOR-1; obstructed by pylon.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, but due to the far northern latitude, the longitude range could greatly vary via swpc_cat tool. High confidence in the latitude around N79, though. Half width less than 10 degrees.
SHOCK analysis. Well-triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2, with a notably wider half width of 53 degrees.

BULK measurement update. Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Halo signature in LASCO C2/C3 is notably dimpled and non-circular, especially compared to STEREO A COR2. This analysis is the best fit of the bulk material excluding a southward nose that travels slightly faster than the main front.
Low confidence, best-fit measurement based on imagery from SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 as well as possible visible source.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 as well as potential source location in SWPC_CAT.
Updated bulk measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 using backfilled SOHO LASCO imagery that was previously unavailable.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. This measurement is consistent with the heavy northern deflection seen in UV imagery of the source eruption.

Analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). This analysis is confirmed by the location of the likely CME source - the narrow northwest directed filament west of AR 4207.

Very approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). There seems to be significant deflection NW, which can be explained by the effect of a small coronal hole south of the associated AR.
Very uncertain analysis based on the location of the source filament and on the fit with two coronagraphs: SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
UPDATE: Reevaluated to be the SHOCK portion of this CME. Measurement based of fainter, wider, outermost leading edge of the CME.
BULK measurement of the brighter, inner portion of the CME. SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to STEREO A COR2 data gap.
Measurement based on source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, two frames of SOHO LASCO C3, and three frames of GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to faintness of event.

Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2. This eruption is in close proximity to a small coronal hole centered at N21W05 as seen in SDO AIA 193, which is directly adjacent and to the southwest of AR 14207 (centered at N29E10); this very likely contributed to the strong northwestward deflection of this eruption.
Best fit measurement using several frames of STEREO A COR2 and a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3. The leading edge was somewhat visible, but too diffuse to reliably track in GOES CCOR-1.
Best fit measurement using a few frames of SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2. The leading edge was somewhat visible, but too diffuse to reliably track in GOES CCOR-1.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK analysis. Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C3 only, and STEREO A COR2.

BULK analysis. Triangulated measurement betweeen SOHO LASCO C3 only, and STEREO A COR2. Leading edge is dimpled and diffuse, so some uncertainty lies in the longitude and could range W95-W115, but good confidence in the far-sided west limb.
Triangulation between STEREO A COR2 and only later frames of SOHO LASCO C3 due to the evolving CME shape through the coronagraphs being difficult to track in LASCO C2.
Parameters based on best fit. Higher uncertainty due to diffuse leading edge.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). Analysis indicates that this is a far-side event. It is not confirmed by the source, which was not found and is likely on the far side.
Approximage analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source has not been found in EUV imagery and is likely far-sided.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

STEREO A COR2 only 3D measurement, as it is too faint in GOES CCOR-1 running difference imagery and not in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 yet due to a data gap. Longitude could easily range W150-W170 with the estimated location using low latency Solar Orbiter STIX and EUI instruments.

STEREO A COR2 only 3D measurement, as it is too faint in GOES CCOR-1 running difference imagery and not in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 yet due to a data gap. Longitude could easily range W150-W170 with the estimated location using low latency Solar Orbiter STIX and EUI instruments.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best-fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

A very speculative analysis based on the fit of an extremely faint front with SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. No clear source has been found but the fit suggest that the source is far-sided, behind the west limb as seen from STEREO A.

Very approximate single-coronagraph analysis (with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 coronagraph) based on the location of the CME source (a narrow ejecta from AR 14207). The CME is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 and STEREO A COR2. The CME is clearly deflecting northward.
Measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on best fit between STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. CME half width is less than 10 degrees.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs and confirmed by the source (flare to the W of AR 14207). The CME seems to deflect northward, just as the associated ejecta seen in EUV imagery. Deflection is likely due to the small coronal hole just south of this Active Region.
Very approximate analysis of the complex front based on the fit with SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1 coronagraphs, and on the location of the source eruption.

Measurement based on potential source location on the limb. The longitude is approximate and may vary by approximately +/- 10 degrees. The leading edge of the CME fades away quickly in running difference imagery and is not measurable beyond a few frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and few available frames of CME before front fades. CME exhibits a strong Northern deflection.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 of the brighest piece of this faint front, the outermost leading edge is too faint and diffuse to track. Front becomes too faint after earliest frames.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible far-sided source.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1.

Measurement based on source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. Although this eruption was visible in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery on the East limb of STEREO A, the CME is not discernable in STEREO A COR2.
Approximate analysis based on the source location as seen in STEREO A EUVI 304/195 and on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2).
Approximate analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1) and confirmed by the location of the likely source (former AR 14207).
Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source seems to be far-sided/behind the E limb.

Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The source seems to be far-sided, based on the coronal signatures (restructuring of magnetic field behind the NE limb in GOES SUVI). Only two STEREO A COR2 images were available for this analysis.

Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3, a few frames of STEREO A COR2, and a few frames of GOES CCOR-1. STEREO A COR2 imagery was at a reduced cadence at the time of this eruption.
Best fit measurement using frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3, two frames of STEREO A COR2, and two frames of GOES CCOR-1. STEREO A COR2 was at a reduced cadence due to a campaign.

Best fit measurement of the faint leading edge using the candidate source location and several frames of SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and two frames of STEREO A COR2. Obstructed by the pylon in GOES CCOR-1. STEREO A COR2 imagery was at a reduced cadence at the time of this eruption.
Measurement based on best-fit among SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2, and GOES CCOR-1 as well as visible source.
Triangulated measurement with SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, CME becomes too faint within LASCO C2 and a STEREO A COR2 data gap begins at 2025-09-17T17:08Z after which the CME is not visible.

Triangulated measurement with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and a single frame of STEREO A COR2 at 2025-09-16T17:08Z due to a data gap after which the CME is not visible. Triangulation assisted by GOES SUVI source observations.

Triangulated measurement between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. Reduced data cadence in STEREO A COR2 and diffuse CME front increases uncertainty, but the longitude likely ranges ~E70-E80 based on concurrent GOES SUVI observations.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. The front of this CME is faint and difficult to track in GOES CCOR-1 and unable to be tracked in SOHO LASCO C3.
Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT as well as potential source location.
A very approximate analysis based on the fit of this faint fuzzy front in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. No source has been found in EUV imagery to confirm parameters.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2) and not confirmed by source (which has not been found).
Very uncertain analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). The longitude could be lower (~135 deg.)
Leading edge measurement mainly relying on imagery in STEREO A COR2A but with SOHO LASCO C2 data up to 2025-09-18T16:24Z.
Leading edge measurement using STEREO A COR2 and SOHO C2 for triangulation; some eastward deflection was noted in the leading edge relative to the supposed source.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 using later frames only, as the CME front shape widens and changes shape in early COR2A and LASCO C2 frames.
Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 with early frames as the diffuse front is difficult to track beyond this.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs. No clear source has been found for this CME.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found to confirm parameters.
Very approximate analysis based on the fit of the faint fuzzy front with 3 coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source found to confirm these parameters.

A very uncertain analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). No source has been found for this CME in available EUV imagery to confirm this analysis.
Updated measurement including STEREO A COR2A imagery.
Very approximate  Analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1). There seems to be a bit of a westward deflection from the source location.
Updated measurement with additional imagery.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. As the source is likely far-sided, a precise source location is unknown. The measured parameters may vary slightly as a result.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and COR2A.
Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2. There is no GOES CCOR-1 imagery for this event due to an eclipse.
Single spacecraft 3D measurement with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 only due to no visibility in STEREO A COR2, using the observed source near S15W52 as the basis and finding a good fit at these parameters.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. Due to uncertainty in the source location, the measured parameters may vary slightly.
Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The measured parameters may vary slightly due to uncertainty in the source location.

Best fit measurement using candidate source location, two frames of SOHO LASCO C2, and one frame STEREO A COR2. There is some uncertainty with this measurement due to the faintness of the leading edge and a data gap in SOHO LASCO lasting from 2025-09-24T09:24Z to 2025-09-24T12:00Z.
Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

Measured longitude based on the source location. The measured parameters may vary slightly due to a data gap in STEREO A COR2 imagery at the time of this event, preventing a stereoscopic measurement from being made.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). No source has been found for this CME in available EUV imagery.
Analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs and confirmed by the source seen in GOES SUVI and STEREO A EUV imagery near S17W75Z. Note that GOES CCOR-1 was not producing a good fit.

Somewhat speculative analysis based on the fit with all three coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO, STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1), however since the CME is very faint and no clear source has been found for it we have no certainty that the narrow faint jet front we see S in STEREO A COR2 is the same jet we see SW in two other coronagraphs.
Best fit measurement using several frames of SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2.

Best fit measurement using source location seen in UV imagery with some southeastward deflection, several frames of SOHO LASCO C2, one frame of SOHO LASCO C3, and one frame of STEREO A COR2. Leading edge becomes too diffuse to track a few frames into the SOHO LASCO C3 field of view.

Analysis based on the fit of this narrow jet with SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 and in alignment with the possible source - a C class flare from AR14232. No good fit with this analysis for CCOR-1.
Analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs, confirming AR 14232 as a likely source.
Corrected analysis based on the fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2).

Analysis including both the southern and the northern lobe of this CME and based on a fit with two coronagraphs (SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2). GOES CCOR-1 coronagraph (specifically the northern lobe of this CME as seen from CCOR-1) does not fit with this analysis.
Approximate analysis based on the fit with SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 coronagraphs.  No source has been found for this CME.
Parameters based on best fit using SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.
